meta.title: GONR Agent-Brand Crosswalk
meta.description: Maps generic dry cleaning agent categories to specific branded products across all 10 profiled companies. When a GONR protocol calls for a generic agent type, this crosswalk tells operators which specific product to reach for based on which company's products they stock.

NSD.description: pH-neutral wet-side spotter and detergent. The universal starting point for water-soluble stain removal. Safe for all fabrics including silk, wool, and delicates due to neutral pH (6.5-7.5). Every spotting board should have one.
NSD.mechanism: Surfactants lower surface tension of water, allowing it to wet and penetrate fibers. The neutral pH avoids damaging pH-sensitive fibers (silk, wool, acetate). Dissolves and suspends water-soluble soils for removal by flushing.

products.notes: Premium quality NSD with fine wetting and penetration additives. Reference-quality neutral spotter. Compatible with all solvent systems.
Budget-friendly neutral wet-side spotter. Functional NSD at a value price point.
Positioned as a delicate fabric spotter rather than a generic NSD, but functions as a neutral wet-side agent safe for silk, rayon, wool, linen, cotton.
Kreussler's detergent line (not a standalone NSD spotter). CLIP COMBI is a universal dry cleaning detergent compatible with all solvent systems.
Detergent family, not standalone NSD. System-specific: HCR (perc), KW (hydrocarbon), Fresh DF (premium HC).
All-in-one detergents with NSD-like cleaning action built in. Not standalone spotters.
Wet cleaning color detergent. Functions as NSD in the Care 4.0 wet cleaning system.
D5-compatible detergents. Not standalone NSD spotters — GreenEarth operators use third-party spotters (A.L. Wilson, R.R. Street).
NCL does not produce dry cleaning NSD products. Institutional/commercial focus.
A.L. Wilson does not market a standalone NSD. Their product line focuses on stain-specific spotters (QwikGo, BonGo, TarGo) rather than a generic neutral agent.

POG.description: Volatile dry-side spotter for non-polar stains — cooking oil, motor oil, tar, adhesive, cosmetics, paint, shoe polish, wax, varnish. Must evaporate completely (volatile) to avoid leaving rings. Applied on the dry side of the spotting board.
POG.mechanism: Dissolves non-polar (oil-based) soil molecules through 'like dissolves like' — the solvent in the POG is chemically similar to the oil in the stain, allowing it to dissolve and carry the stain away. Controlled evaporation rate is critical: too fast and the solvent leaves before dissolving the stain, too slow and it leaves solvent rings.

products.notes: Volatile dry spotter with controlled evaporation. Can be combined with STREETEX® to create 2-1 FORMULA® (wet/dry combo for mystery stains).
TarGo® is water-based (modern, eco-friendly). TarGo Dry® is traditional dry-side. Laundry TarGo® contains orange terpenes for laundry pre-spotting.

products.productName: PRENETT C (Green) / DEPRIT GREEN / DEPRIT WHITE
products.notes: PRENETT C: neutral pH pre-spotter for fats/makeup (10-20 min dwell). DEPRIT GREEN: neutral post-spotter for oil/grease/adhesive. DEPRIT WHITE: surfactant-free solvent blend for stubborn non-polar soils.
Standard solvent-based dry-side spotter. Straightforward POG function.
Perc-based pre/post-spotter for paint, lacquer, adhesives, ballpoint pen, tar, nail polish, lipstick, ink, grease.
Claims no rings, no streaks, no swales, no graying. Designed for even feathering on dark fabrics.
Multi-purpose spotter with oil removal capability. Also functions as wet cleaning pre-spotter.
Part of Büfa's multi-variant spotting system.
No GreenEarth-branded POG. Use R.R. Street PICRIN® or A.L. Wilson TarGo® in GreenEarth systems.
Carpet-grade petroleum spotter. NOT for garment use.

protein.description: Wet-side agent for protein-based stains — blood, milk, egg, perspiration, food, baby formula, urine, grass. Protein stains coagulate (set) with heat, so treatment must use cool or lukewarm chemistry first. Some protein spotters contain enzymes (protease) for deeper penetration — these are MORE effective but NEVER safe for silk or wool because enzymes digest protein fibers.
protein.mechanism: Surfactant-based protein spotters break the hydrogen bonds holding coagulated protein to fiber surfaces. Enzyme-based versions (protease) go further — they cleave peptide bonds in the protein molecule itself, literally digesting the stain. This is why enzymes work better on old/set stains but destroy silk (silk IS protein).
protein.safetyWarning: ENZYME-CONTAINING PROTEIN SPOTTERS MUST NEVER BE USED ON SILK, WOOL, CASHMERE, OR ANY PROTEIN-BASED FIBER. Protease enzymes cannot distinguish between stain protein and fiber protein. Always check the product label for enzyme content.

products.notes: QwikGo®: safe for all fabrics including delicates. SoGo '1'®: enzyme-activated, more aggressive, NOT for silk/wool. SoGo withstands 145°F for warm-water enzyme activation.
Quick-acting protein spotter. Can alternate with StreeTAN® for stubborn combination stains (pH shift technique).
PRENETT B: alkaline (pH 9.5-10.5) pre-spotter. DEPRIT RED: alkaline post-spotter. WARNING: alkaline pH requires caution on silk (breaks peptide bonds) and wool (damages keratin).
Wetspot: ammonia-based, NOT for silk/wool. BPR: targeted blood/protein. RSR: powdered enzyme digester, NOT for silk/wool.
Safe for delicate colors and fabrics. Broad target range including blood, perspiration, milk, eggs, glue, gravy, ketchup, vomit, urine, chocolate.
Universal pre-spotter that handles protein among other stain types. Not a dedicated protein-only agent.
Eco-friendly formula with enzymes. NOT safe for silk/wool due to enzyme content.
Letter-coded system — specific variants target protein stains.
No GreenEarth-branded protein spotter. Use A.L. Wilson QwikGo® or R.R. Street StreePRO®.
Enzyme-based but designed for drains/hard surfaces, NOT for textile use.

tannin.description: Acidic wet-side agent for plant polyphenol stains — coffee, tea, wine, beer, fruit juice, grass, soft drinks, mustard, cola. Tannins bond to textile fibers through hydrogen bonding; acidic chemistry disrupts those bonds to release the stain.
tannin.mechanism: Acid (typically organic acids like gluconic acid or citric acid, or stronger mineral acid formulations) protonates the hydroxyl groups on tannin polyphenols, breaking the hydrogen bonds that anchor the tannin to fiber surfaces. The freed tannin molecules dissolve in the acidic solution and flush away.

products.notes: StreeTAN®: heat-stable (activity doesn't increase with temperature — unique safety feature for delicates). No cold-water pre-rinse needed before steam flushing. 209®: more aggressive, for experienced spotters.
Gluconic acid base — mild enough for delicates but effective on most tannin stains. Safe for delicate colors and fabrics. Removes virtually all beverage stains except milk (which is protein).
PRENETT A: pH 3.0-4.0 acidic pre-spotter. Blue color coding. DEPRIT BLUE: acidic post-spotter for residual tannin stains.
Booster used WITH a primary tannin agent — not a standalone tannin spotter.
Specifically targets grass, tea, coffee, and tannin stains. Part of the lettered Benzafix system.
Universal pre-spotters that handle tannin among other stain types. Not dedicated tannin-only agents.
No dedicated tannin spotter identified in Royaltone's product line.
No dedicated tannin spotter identified in current public product catalog.
No GreenEarth-branded tannin spotter. Use R.R. Street StreeTAN® or A.L. Wilson BonGo®.
Carpet-grade organic acid tannin remover. NOT for garment use — formulated for carpet durability, not fabric safety.

leveling.description: Prevents water rings and marks on moisture-sensitive fabrics (silk, rayon, acetate). Applied before or during wet-side spotting to control how moisture spreads through fibers. Essential when working with any water-based chemistry on water-sensitive garments.
leveling.mechanism: Leveling agents contain surfactants and wetting agents that reduce surface tension of water, causing it to spread evenly through the fabric rather than creating a defined wet boundary. The ring/mark forms at the boundary where wet meets dry — by eliminating that sharp boundary, leveling agents prevent visible marks.

products.notes: Purpose-built for water rings on silk and rayon. Both prevents and removes water marks. Functions as both spotter and leveling agent.
Pre-treatment/leveling agents. MultiSPOT® for perc, GreenSPOT® for GreenEarth. No-flush application.
Fixing and leveling agent. Stabilizes direct dyes and prevents redeposition in colored wash.
No dedicated leveling agent in A.L. Wilson's product line.
No dedicated leveling agent identified.
Neutra-Lube's neutral pH and premium wetting additives provide some leveling function on delicates, though it's not marketed as a leveling agent.
Seitz references leveling agents in their product line but specific product name not confirmed in public materials.
Functions as a refiner/conditioner in the Care 4.0 wet cleaning system. Not a standalone spotting board leveling agent.
Use R.R. Street GreenSPOT® in GreenEarth systems.
Not applicable — institutional cleaning focus.

rustRemover.description: Acid-based agent for iron oxide (rust) stains from metal contact, iron-rich water, or oxidized blood. Typically contains oxalic acid or hydrofluoric acid. Aggressive chemistry that requires quick application, rapid flushing, and careful handling.
rustRemover.mechanism: Strong acids dissolve iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) by converting it to soluble iron salts. Oxalic acid forms soluble iron oxalate complexes. Hydrofluoric acid (in some formulations) is extremely effective but extremely dangerous. The acid must be neutralized after treatment to prevent ongoing fiber damage.
rustRemover.safetyWarning: Rust removers contain strong acids. Always wear chemical-resistant gloves and eye protection. Apply only to the stain — do not let the agent spread. Flush quickly. Neutralize after treatment. Never use on natural stone surfaces that may be near the spotting board.

products.notes: Fast-acting rust remover. Standard professional rust removal agent.
Acidic post-spotter for rust, metal stains, old blood, and ointments. Part of the DEPRIT Professional six-product system.
Multi-purpose formula — also handles lacquer, nail polish, shellac, adhesive, varnish, grass, gum, ink, cosmetics. Broader than a dedicated rust-only agent.
No dedicated rust remover identified in current Street's product line.
Not identified in public product information.
No rust remover in product line.
Not identified in current product catalog.
Likely available within the SpotX seven-variant system but not specifically confirmed.
No branded rust remover.
No textile-grade rust remover.

enzymatic.description: Contains protease enzymes that physically digest protein-based stain molecules. More effective than surfactant-based protein spotters on old, set, or embedded protein stains because enzymes break peptide bonds in the protein structure itself. CRITICAL SAFETY RULE: Never use on silk, wool, cashmere, or any protein-based fiber.
enzymatic.mechanism: Protease enzymes are biological catalysts that cleave peptide bonds in protein chains. They 'eat' the stain protein one bond at a time. This is why enzyme spotters work on stains that surfactant-based agents can't fully remove — the enzyme breaks down protein at a molecular level, while surfactants only work at the surface. The enzyme continues working as long as it has substrate (protein) and appropriate conditions (pH, temperature).
enzymatic.safetyWarning: ENZYMES CANNOT DISTINGUISH BETWEEN STAIN PROTEIN AND FIBER PROTEIN. Silk (fibroin), wool (keratin), and cashmere (keratin) are all protein fibers. Protease enzymes will digest these fibers just as effectively as they digest blood or food proteins. THIS IS THE MOST COMMON CAUSE OF ENZYME DAMAGE IN PROFESSIONAL CLEANING.

products.notes: Enzyme-activated synthetic detergent digestant. Withstands 145°F. Use as board spotter or soak. NOT for silk/wool.
Cold-active enzyme pre-brushing agent for collar soil and heavy dirt. Part of wet cleaning system. NOT for silk/wool.
Powdered enzyme product. Dissolve in water for soak applications. NOT for silk/wool.
Eco-friendly formula containing enzymes plus solvents and detergents. NOT for silk/wool.
No dedicated enzyme product in current Street's product line.
Enzymatic agents referenced in product line but specific product not identified.
No enzyme product identified.
Enzyme capability implied but not specifically confirmed as standalone product.
No enzyme product.
Bio-enzymatic cleaners for drains/hard surfaces. NOT formulated for textile safety.

solvent.description: The primary cleaning medium in a dry cleaning machine. Dissolves and carries away oil-based soils from garments. Choice of solvent determines the entire cleaning system — detergents, spotters, and equipment must all be compatible.
solvent.mechanism: Solvents dissolve non-polar (oil-based) soil through 'like dissolves like' — the solvent's molecular structure is similar enough to oil-based soils to dissolve them. The dissolved soil is carried to a filter and/or distillation unit, and the clean solvent is recycled back through the garments.

products.notes: Synthetic High Flash Hydrocarbon by ExxonMobil, distributed by Street's. Lowest aromatic content of any HFHC. Flash point >130°F. The dominant hydrocarbon solvent in the US.
Bio-based acetal from corn. Non-toxic, biodegradable, patent-protected. Only bio-based solvent on the market. Requires SYSTEMK4 equipment.
SENSENE™: modified alcohol, KB 161 (highest non-perc solvency). DC Naturell®: liquid silicone. intense®: enhanced-safety solvent. Plus hydrocarbon. Most solvent options of any company.
Liquid silicone. Non-toxic, chemically inert, gentle. KB ~15 (weakest cleaning power). Licensed system model.
TDC 2000: high-purity isoparaffinic hydrocarbon (C11-C12, narrow fraction). Also distributes SENSENE™.
A.L. Wilson does not manufacture solvents — spotting agent specialist only.
No proprietary solvent. Products designed for use with DF-2000™ or perc.
Does not manufacture dry cleaning solvents.
Does not manufacture dry cleaning solvents.
Does not manufacture dry cleaning solvents.

oxidizingBleach.description: Hydrogen peroxide-based or sodium percarbonate-based bleach that removes color and residual stains through oxidation. Used on white fabrics only — will strip dyes from colored garments. Must be neutralized after use to prevent ongoing fiber damage.
oxidizingBleach.mechanism: Oxidizing agents donate oxygen atoms to chromophore (color-producing) molecular structures in stains, breaking the electronic conjugation that produces visible color. The stain molecule becomes colorless. This same mechanism will attack garment dyes — hence the restriction to white fabrics.

products.notes: Removes residual ink, protein, and tannin stains. White fabric ONLY. MUST neutralize with ColorGo® or ExGo® after treatment.
Kreussler does not prominently market a standalone oxidizing bleach. Their DEPRIT system handles most residual stains through targeted chemistry rather than bleaching.

products.productName: Not identified in current product line
products.notes: Street's product catalog does not include a prominently marketed oxidizing bleach.
Most other companies either don't offer or don't prominently market oxidizing bleach products. Hydrogen peroxide (3%) is often used as a generic oxidizing agent.

reducingAgent.description: Chemical bleach that removes unwanted color by stripping electrons from dye molecules. Used for dye transfer recovery, residual color stains, and as a counterpart to oxidizing bleach. More aggressive and hazardous than oxidizing bleach.
reducingAgent.mechanism: Reducing agents donate electrons to dye molecules, breaking the electronic conjugation in the chromophore. This is the chemical opposite of oxidation — instead of adding oxygen, it removes it. Effective on stains that oxidizing bleach can't touch (some reactive dyes, some set stains).

products.notes: YellowGo®: titanium sulfate-based, contains sulfuric acid — extremely hazardous, full PPE required. The most powerful reducing bleach in professional dry cleaning. ColorGo®: powdered alternative, less aggressive. Also neutralizes PermaGo®.
Kreussler's DEPRIT PURPLE addresses dye/ink stains through alkaline chemistry rather than reducing bleach.
A.L. Wilson is the primary source for professional-grade reducing bleach in the US market.

wetCleaningDetergent.description: Specialized detergent for washing dry-clean-only garments in water. Formulated with mild surfactants that clean without stripping natural fiber oils. pH-controlled to protect protein fibers. Used in conjunction with professional wet cleaning equipment (controlled temperature, limited mechanical action).
wetCleaningDetergent.mechanism: Mild, non-ionic and anionic surfactants create micelles that encapsulate and remove soil without the aggressive action of standard laundry detergents. Fiber protection agents coat fiber surfaces to prevent felting (wool) and weakening (rayon). pH buffers maintain neutral conditions to protect peptide bonds in silk and keratin bonds in wool.

products.notes: Industry standard. Developed with Miele 20+ years ago. Blue Angel certified. Safe for wool, silk, cashmere, delicates.
For hand washing, top-load, front-load, professional laundry, and wetcleaning machines.
Complete wet cleaning product range.
Ozerna Polar: color protection. Ozerna Basic: general textiles and leather.
Multi-purpose wet cleaning detergents in the Adco/Street's portfolio.
Specifically formulated for leather and suede — prevents stiffening. Niche application.
75+ liquid laundry products. More institutional than professional garment wet cleaning.
A.L. Wilson does not manufacture wet cleaning detergents — spotting specialist only.
GreenEarth is a solvent system, not a wet cleaning system.
Institutional laundry detergent. Not professional garment wet cleaning.

finishingAgent.description: Post-cleaning treatment that restores body, crispness, and hand to garments. Essential after wet cleaning (which strips natural fiber stiffness). May also include water/stain repellent treatments, softeners, and anti-static agents.
finishingAgent.mechanism: Sizing agents deposit a thin film of polymer (starch, synthetic polymer, or silicone) on fiber surfaces. This restores the 'hand' (tactile quality) that cleaning processes strip away. Repellent finishes work by coating fiber surfaces with hydrophobic (water-repelling) molecules — traditionally fluorocarbons (PFAS), now increasingly PFAS-free alternatives.

products.productName: LANADOL APRET / HYDROB EASY DRY / VINOYK4
products.notes: APRET: sizing/finishing for wet cleaning. HYDROB EASY DRY: PFOA/PFOS-free water/oil repellent. VINOYK4: stain repellent that also preserves SolvonK4 solvent purity.
Silksheen™: anti-streak finishing aid. Aquasize®: wet cleaning sizing. Softenall®: fabric softener.

products.productName: Secasit Lotus / Lizerna Carat / Lival Licker Conc
products.notes: Secasit Lotus: fluorocarbon-reduced water-proofing. Lizerna Carat: wet cleaning conditioner. Lival Licker Conc: leather conditioning.
Leather/fur finishing agent — restores suppleness and color depth.
Finishing agents available but specific product names not prominent in public materials.
Finishing/water repellent category exists in Street's portfolio. Specific product details limited.
A.L. Wilson, Pariser, Royaltone, GreenEarth, and NCL do not prominently market finishing agents.
